rlaReading.title: Practice 1: Fight Fire with Fire
rlaReading.passageTitle: Fight Fire with Fire: Dual Perspectives
rlaReading.passage1Meta: Guest Column: Fight Fire with Fire by Paula Lehner
rlaReading.passage2Meta: Letter to the Editor: Prescribed Burns by Roy Rodriguez
passage1Paragraphs: Here's an alarming number: $2 billion, the amount the U.S. Forest Service spends each year on wildfire {suppression}. No one is claiming that we should stop fighting these fires, but there is a scientifically proven way to limit their spread: by setting controlled, or {prescribed}, burns. These smaller fires, which clear {accumulated} dead or diseased plant life from forests and other areas, are far less {hazardous} than the uncontrolled wildfires that are increasing in frequency. By regularly burning up decaying {vegetation}, smaller fires eliminate that potential fuel for much larger fires. Less plant-matter fuel on the ground helps slow the progress of unwanted fires before they ever start.

In addition to causing fuel buildup, wildfire suppression has other negative consequences. For example, wildfires can {purge} insect {infestations}. In nature, small fires normally thin the population of insects in an area, but in the absence of those fires there is little to stop insect populations from increasing to unnaturally high levels. Further, many plants developed their life cycles in response to {periodic} natural fires from lightning strikes and other sources; these plants need fire if they are to remain healthy. Burning, as opposed to wildfire suppression, also helps create ash, which returns nutrients to the soil and can help new seeds {germinate}.

Certainly, there will always be fires that we have to fight. People and their homes have to be protected. But the fact that more and more homes are being built near wilderness areas, many right here in Riverton, means that the burden on land management agencies and their brave firefighters is increasing. We need the right tools if we are going to continue to do our jobs effectively, and those tools include prescribed burns.
There is no way around it. We have to fight fire with fire.
passage2Paragraphs: I found Paula Lehner's opinion piece about prescribed burns nothing less than {horrifying}. In her {zeal}, she neglected to mention even one of the many criticisms of prescribed burns. First, consider this {appalling} statistic: one in every 500 prescribed burns gets out of control, and while that may sound like a small {fraction}, just imagine if that one raging fire were the one Lehner had decided to set near your house. In 2009, a "controlled" fire set in Yosemite National Park was intended to burn 91 acres; instead it {consumed} 5,000 acres.

As for her {ridiculous} idea that "controlled" fires are needed to rid areas of wildfire fuel, doesn't she realize that fire isn't the only way to eliminate unwanted plants? Has she not heard of mechanical and chemical thinning-in simple terms, the use of power tools and {herbicides}?

Finally-and this is the worst {omission} of all-she did not mention the costs to the surrounding area of the fires she wants to set. Riverton has, in the past year, hit all-time high air pollution levels, and smoke from Lehner's fires would only worsen those numbers. Has she bothered to research the number of allergy and asthma sufferers in our region? Does she know that, nationwide, the {incidence} of allergies and asthma is rising? Is she planning to personally hand out face masks to affected individuals, or does she have some exciting new smoke-containment technology at her disposal?
Ms. Lehner should do more research before recommending a dangerous path for our community. We must fight the fire, not add fuel to it.

suppression.def: The act of forcibly putting an end to something.
suppression.vn: Sự đàn áp / Sự dập tắt

prescribed.def: Recommended or authorized, typically a controlled action.
prescribed.vn: Được chỉ định / Được kiểm soát
prescribed.vnDef: Hành động có kiểm soát với mục đích cụ thể.

accumulated.def: Gathered or built up over a period of time.
accumulated.vnDef: Tập hợp lại trong một khoảng thời gian.

hazardous.vnDef: Gây nguy hiểm cho sức khỏe hoặc sự an toàn.

vegetation.vnDef: Toàn bộ thực vật mọc trong một khu vực.

purge.vnDef: Dọn sạch những thứ không mong muốn.

infestations.def: The presence of an unusually large number of insects/animals causing damage.
infestations.vnDef: Sự xuất hiện số lượng lớn bất thường của côn trùng.

periodic.vnDef: Xảy ra lặp đi lặp lại.

germinate.def: To begin to grow and put out shoots.
germinate.vnDef: Bắt đầu phát triển và đâm chồi.

zeal.def: Great energy or enthusiasm in pursuit of a cause.
zeal.vn: Sự hăng hái / Lòng nhiệt huyết
zeal.vnDef: Sự nhiệt tình trong việc theo đuổi mục tiêu.

omission.def: Someone or something that has been left out.
omission.vnDef: Thứ gì đó đã bị bỏ quên hoặc loại trừ.

incidence.def: The occurrence, rate, or frequency of a disease or something undesirable.
incidence.vn: Tỷ lệ mắc phải / Tần suất xảy ra
incidence.vnDef: Tỷ lệ hoặc tần suất của một điều không mong muốn.

horrifying.def: Causing much fear, horror, or shock.
horrifying.vnDef: Gây ra sự sợ hãi, kinh dị hoặc sốc.

appalling.vnDef: Gây sốc hoặc mất tinh thần; tồi tệ.

fraction.def: A small or tiny part, amount, or proportion of something.
fraction.vnDef: Một phần nhỏ hoặc tỷ lệ nhỏ của một thứ gì đó.

consumed.vnDef: Bị phá hủy hoàn toàn (đặc biệt là bởi lửa).

ridiculous.def: Deserving or inviting derision or mockery; absurd.
ridiculous.vnDef: Đáng bị chế giễu; vô lý.

herbicides.def: A substance that is toxic to plants, used to destroy unwanted vegetation.
herbicides.vnDef: Chất độc đối với thực vật, dùng để tiêu diệt cỏ dại.

questions.title: 1. What is the impact of Lehner's use of the word "tools" in paragraph 3?

options.text: A. It minimizes the emotional reaction people often have to prescribed burns.
B. It indicates that using prescribed burns makes firefighting similar to other trades.
C. It emphasizes that prescribed burns should be used only under specific conditions.
D. It suggests that prescribed burns have costs similar to other implements used by firefighters.

questions.expEn: By calling prescribed burns 'tools', Lehner normalizes them. People usually view fires as terrifying or destructive disasters, but calling them 'tools' strips away that emotion and makes them sound like necessary, safe, and standard professional equipment.
questions.expVn: Bằng cách gọi các đám cháy có kiểm soát là 'công cụ' (tools), Lehner bình thường hóa chúng. Mọi người thường coi hỏa hoạn là thảm họa đáng sợ, nhưng từ 'công cụ' loại bỏ cảm xúc đó và khiến chúng nghe có vẻ giống như thiết bị chuyên dụng, cần thiết và an toàn.

questions.title: 2. What evidence does Lehner use to support her claims? Drag and drop two pieces of evidence for each claim.

bank.text: [A] More homes are being built near wilderness areas.
[B] Small fires thin populations of insects.
[C] Uncontrolled wildfires are becoming more frequent.
[D] Prescribed burns can limit wildfires' spread.
[E] Some plants develop by means of lightning strikes.
[F] Land managers need certain tools for their jobs.

claims.text: Claim 1: The burden on firefighters is increasing.

questions.expEn: Claim 1 is supported by [A] and [C] because more homes in the wilderness and more frequent fires create more work (burden) for firefighters (Paragraphs 1 & 3). Claim 2 is supported by [B] and [E] because paragraph 2 explicitly lists thinning insects and helping plant life cycles as natural benefits of fire.
questions.expVn: Nhận định 1 được hỗ trợ bởi [A] và [C] vì việc có nhiều nhà ở khu vực hoang dã hơn và cháy rừng thường xuyên hơn tạo ra nhiều gánh nặng công việc hơn cho lính cứu hỏa (Đoạn 1 & 3). Nhận định 2 được hỗ trợ bởi [B] và [E] vì đoạn 2 liệt kê rõ ràng việc giảm lượng côn trùng và giúp ích cho chu kỳ sống của thực vật là những lợi ích tự nhiên của lửa.

questions.title: 3. Which detail in Rodriguez's letter supports the idea that there are disadvantages associated with controlled fires?

options.text: A. "... fire isn't the only way to eliminate unwanted plants..." (paragraph 6)
B. "Has she not heard of mechanical and chemical thinning..." (paragraph 6)
C. "Riverton has, in the past year, hit all-time high air pollution levels, and smoke from Lehner's fires would only worsen those numbers." (paragraph 7)
D. "... nationwide, the incidence of allergies and asthma is rising..." (paragraph 7)

questions.expEn: Option C provides a direct, specific negative consequence (disadvantage) of the controlled fires themselves: worsening the local air pollution. Options A and B talk about alternatives, and D states a general fact without directly linking it back to the fires in that exact sentence.
questions.expVn: Lựa chọn C cung cấp một hậu quả tiêu cực trực tiếp, cụ thể (nhược điểm) của chính các đám cháy có kiểm soát: làm tồi tệ thêm tình trạng ô nhiễm không khí ở địa phương.

questions.title: 4. How does Rodriguez build his argument that "Lehner should do more research"?

options.text: A. Rodriguez brings up several issues relevant to prescribed burns that Lehner fails to address.
B. Rodriguez repeatedly casts doubt on Lehner's qualifications for discussing the issue of prescribed burns.
C. Rodriguez raises numerous questions about whether Lehner's statistics about prescribed burns are accurate.
D. Rodriguez demonstrates that Lehner is an outsider to the community that would be affected by prescribed burns.

questions.expEn: Rodriguez attacks Lehner's argument by pointing out what she 'neglected to mention' or made a 'worst omission of all' (pollution, asthma, alternative methods). By listing these unaddressed issues, he implies she hasn't researched the topic fully.
questions.expVn: Rodriguez công kích lập luận của Lehner bằng cách chỉ ra những gì cô ấy 'đã phớt lờ không nhắc đến' hoặc 'sự bỏ sót tồi tệ nhất' (ô nhiễm, hen suyễn, các phương pháp thay thế). Bằng cách liệt kê những vấn đề chưa được giải quyết này, ông ngụ ý rằng cô ấy chưa nghiên cứu đầy đủ về chủ đề này.

questions.title: 5. Which idea about the effect of prescribed burns is included in Rodriguez's letter?

options.text: A. They result in decreased sales of power tools and herbicides.
B. They result in reduced enthusiasm about fighting air pollution.
C. They cause financial hardship for organizations that fight fires.
D. They cause respiratory problems for increasing numbers of people.

questions.expEn: In paragraph 7, Rodriguez specifically mentions that the smoke from the fires affects allergy and asthma sufferers, which are respiratory problems. The other options are not mentioned in the text.
questions.expVn: Trong đoạn 7, Rodriguez đặc biệt đề cập rằng khói từ các đám cháy ảnh hưởng đến những người bị dị ứng và hen suyễn (đây là các vấn đề về hô hấp). Các lựa chọn khác không được đề cập trong văn bản.

questions.title: 6. Which conclusion is supported by Rodriguez's letter?

options.text: A. Prescribed burns have caused damage to residential areas.
B. The size of prescribed burns and the smoke they create are difficult to control.
C. Mechanical and chemical thinning are more successful than fire at eliminating unwanted plants.
D. Inventing new smoke-containment technologies would increase the effectiveness of prescribed burns.

questions.expEn: Rodriguez provides evidence that fires meant for 91 acres can consume 5,000 acres instead (size is hard to control), and he complains about the widespread, uncontainable smoke causing air pollution (smoke is hard to control).
questions.expVn: Rodriguez cung cấp bằng chứng cho thấy các đám cháy dự kiến rộng 91 mẫu Anh có thể thiêu rụi tới 5.000 mẫu Anh (kích thước khó kiểm soát), và ông phàn nàn về lượng khói lan rộng không thể ngăn chặn gây ô nhiễm không khí (khói khó kiểm soát).

questions.title: 7. How are the conclusions of Lehner's column and Rodriguez's letter similar?

options.text: A. Both convey the idea that firefighters should have a say in the use of prescribed burns.
B. Both convey the idea that prescribed burns are simply one element of wildfire prevention.
C. Both convey the idea that the subject of prescribed burns leaves no room for compromise.
D. Both convey the idea that the effectiveness of prescribed burns has not yet been sufficiently studied.

questions.expEn: Both authors use absolute, stubborn language in their final sentences. Lehner says, 'There is no way around it. We have to fight fire with fire.' Rodriguez says, 'We must fight the fire, not add fuel to it.' Both show they are completely unwilling to compromise on their opposing stances.
questions.expVn: Cả hai tác giả đều sử dụng ngôn từ tuyệt đối, cứng rắn trong câu chốt của họ. Lehner nói, 'Không có cách nào khác. Chúng ta phải lấy độc trị độc.' Rodriguez nói, 'Chúng ta phải chiến đấu với ngọn lửa, chứ không phải thêm dầu vào lửa.' Cả hai đều cho thấy họ hoàn toàn không sẵn sàng thỏa hiệp với lập trường trái ngược của mình.

rlaReading.title: Practice 2: Game-Based Learning
rlaReading.passage1Meta: Game-based Learning Is Merely a Fad by Solomon Robles, Ed.D.
rlaReading.passage2Meta: Game-based Learning: An Effective Training Strategy by Janette Morgan, Ph.D.
passage1Paragraphs: Electronic learning, or "eLearning," was sold to many CEOs as the wave of the {corporate} future. There's no need to hire human beings to train staff, managers were told. All the information you require can be accessed online at a reduced cost for the company, and more {flexible} scheduling for employees. It sounded too good to be true and it was. As one writer noted, "most eLearning is nothing more than online lectures or course notes." To teach skills beyond basic facts, the person-to-person method is still more effective than any form of electronic learning.

And yet, along comes the next eLearning {fad}: "{gamification}," which means using video games to teach. Video-game-based learning is already widely used in schools, and has spread to corporations as a tool for training workers and motivating customers. This approach is even used in the military. But does it work?

Most research on game-based learning has looked at schools. Results have been {inconsistent} but not especially encouraging. One 2013 study found that "students who completed the gamified experience got better scores in practical assignments and in overall score, but... performed poorly on written assignments and participated less during class activities." This makes sense: If you're playing video games, you are not developing the so-called "{soft skills}" - also known as "people skills" - that are necessary for success in any job. In a traditional classroom, on the other hand, students can ask questions and engage with the teacher and with other learners.

In the workplace, game-based learning is yet another {wedge} driven between younger employees, who might applaud gamification, and older workers, who may now feel {obsolete}. What's more, the {prestigious} magazine The Economist points out that "many of the aspects of gamification that do work are merely old ideas in {trendy} new clothes." For example, the points and other rewards that are selling features of video games are just online versions of sales contests or "employee of the month" {perks}. Like other trends, game-based learning is likely to fade away as the next new teaching fad appears on the horizon.
passage2Paragraphs: Already a multi-billion-dollar business, game-based learning in the corporate world continues to grow at a steady pace, and is here to stay. {Executives} are smart - they are not going to waste resources on training methods that don't work. Let's look at some of the advantages of "gamifying" workplace training.

First, many people are "gamers" in their non-work lives, so playing video games is something they are already comfortable with and enjoy. This makes game-based learning in the workplace more attractive and motivating than traditional instruction. And according to the Entertainment Software Association, despite the image of video-game players as teenaged boys, "the average gamer is now 37 years old." Further challenging the {stereotype}, nearly half of gamers are girls or women. Even 29 percent of those over age 50 are getting into the video-game habit. Teenagers and young men, in fact, make up only 15 percent of the over 190 million video-game users in the United States.

Evidence supporting the effectiveness of game-based learning is starting to emerge. Researchers point out that video games have "{compelling} storylines, {attainable} challenges, rewards, recognition and control," all of which {stimulate} learners. A 2012 report on game-based learning notes that "there is research evidence demonstrating positive impact on higher order skills such as decision making and problem solving." The report adds that using video games can also reduce training time, an advantage for both managers and employees.

Finally, unlike one-time training in a classroom, game-based learning is {infinitely} repeatable. If employees miss something or need more practice, they can always start the game again, using the feedback provided by the game to {gauge} their progress. This leads to a sense of {accomplishment} and creates a supportive learning environment, which is what we all want in an education strategy.

fad.def: A popular trend that quickly fades away.
fad.vnDef: Một xu hướng rất phổ biến nhưng nhanh chóng bị lãng quên.

corporate.def: Related to a large business or company.
corporate.vnDef: Liên quan đến các công ty hoặc doanh nghiệp lớn.

flexible.vnDef: Dễ dàng thay đổi để phù hợp với hoàn cảnh.

gamification.def: Turning tasks into games to make them fun.
gamification.vnDef: Biến công việc hoặc bài học thành trò chơi để trở nên hấp dẫn hơn.

inconsistent.def: Changing frequently; not the same all the time.
inconsistent.vnDef: Hay thay đổi, không giống nhau mọi lúc.

soft skills.def: People skills, like communication and teamwork.
soft skills.vnDef: Kỹ năng giao tiếp và làm việc với người khác.

obsolete.def: No longer useful because it is too old.
obsolete.vnDef: Đã quá cũ và không còn được sử dụng nữa.

prestigious.vnDef: Rất nổi tiếng và được mọi người tôn trọng.

trendy.vnDef: Rất phổ biến và thời thượng hiện nay.

perks.def: Extra benefits or rewards from a job.
perks.vnDef: Những phần thưởng hoặc lợi ích thêm từ công việc ngoài tiền lương.

executive.def: A top boss or leader in a company.
executive.vnDef: Người sếp hoặc lãnh đạo cấp cao trong một công ty.

stereotype.def: A common but often false belief about a group of people.
stereotype.vnDef: Một niềm tin phổ biến nhưng thường không đúng về một nhóm người.

compelling.def: Very interesting and holding your attention.
compelling.vnDef: Rất thú vị và thu hút sự chú ý của bạn.

attainable.vnDef: Khả thi, có thể làm được hoặc đạt được.

stimulate.vnDef: Khuyến khích sự hào hứng hoặc làm cho điều gì đó hoạt động.

infinitely.vnDef: Không có giới hạn; có thể lặp đi lặp lại vô tận.

gauge.vnDef: Đo lường hoặc đánh giá mức độ của một việc gì đó.

accomplishment.vnDef: Một thành công hoặc một mục tiêu đã hoàn thành.

wedge.def: Something that separates people or causes problems between them.
wedge.vnDef: Thứ gây chia rẽ hoặc tạo khoảng cách giữa mọi người.

questions.title: 1. What is the primary argument made by Solomon Robles in Source Material #1?

options.text: A. Gamification is an excellent way to replace traditional classroom teachers.
B. Video games are only appropriate for teenage students and should not be used in the workplace.
C. Game-based learning is a passing trend that fails to teach the necessary soft skills developed in traditional classrooms.
D. Electronic learning is too expensive and inflexible for modern corporations to implement.

questions.expEn: Robles argues that gamification is just another 'fad' (trend) and cites a study showing it leads to poor performance on written assignments. He emphasizes that video games do not develop the 'soft skills' or 'people skills' that are naturally built in traditional classrooms where students engage with others.
questions.expVn: Robles lập luận rằng 'trò chơi hóa' (gamification) chỉ là một trào lưu nhất thời (fad) và trích dẫn một nghiên cứu cho thấy nó dẫn đến kết quả kém trong các bài tập viết. Ông nhấn mạnh rằng trò chơi điện tử không phát triển được 'kỹ năng mềm' hay 'kỹ năng giao tiếp' - những kỹ năng được rèn luyện tự nhiên trong các lớp học truyền thống khi học sinh tương tác với người khác.

questions.title: 2. In Source Material #2, how does Janette Morgan challenge the stereotype that gamers are mostly "teenaged boys"?

options.text: A. By arguing that game-based learning is a multi-billion-dollar business.
B. By citing data showing the average gamer is 37 years old and nearly half are female.
C. By proving that older workers learn faster than younger workers when using video games.
D. By stating that 29 percent of teenagers prefer traditional instruction over video games.

questions.expEn: Morgan directly counters the stereotype by providing statistics from the Entertainment Software Association. She notes that 'the average gamer is now 37 years old,' 'nearly half of gamers are girls or women,' and teenagers/young men only make up 15 percent of the user base.
questions.expVn: Morgan trực tiếp bác bỏ định kiến này bằng cách đưa ra số liệu thống kê từ Hiệp hội Phần mềm Giải trí. Cô lưu ý rằng 'độ tuổi trung bình của người chơi game hiện là 37', 'gần một nửa số người chơi là nữ giới', và thanh thiếu niên/nam thanh niên chỉ chiếm 15% tổng số người dùng.

questions.title: 3. In Source Material #1, paragraph 4, what does the word "obsolete" most likely mean?

options.text: A. Highly valued and respected
B. Confused and angry
C. Trendy and fashionable
D. Outdated and no longer useful

questions.expEn: Robles writes that game-based learning drives a wedge between younger employees and older workers, 'who may now feel obsolete.' Because gamification is a new, trendy technology, older workers might feel left behind, outdated, or no longer useful to the company.
questions.expVn: Robles viết rằng phương pháp học qua game tạo ra khoảng cách giữa nhân viên trẻ và những nhân viên lớn tuổi, 'những người giờ đây có thể cảm thấy lỗi thời (obsolete)'. Vì trò chơi hóa là một công nghệ mới và hợp thời, những người lao động lớn tuổi có thể cảm thấy bị tụt hậu, lỗi thời hoặc không còn hữu ích cho công ty.

questions.title: 4. On which of the following points would Robles and Morgan most likely DISAGREE?

options.text: A. The fact that game-based learning is currently being used by major corporations.
B. The effectiveness of game-based learning in training employees and developing essential skills.
C. The idea that traditional classrooms involve person-to-person engagement.
D. The reality that electronic learning methods were introduced to reduce company costs.

questions.expEn: Both authors agree that gamification is currently being used in corporations (A), involves non-traditional methods (C), and was historically meant to save money (D). However, they strongly disagree on its effectiveness. Morgan believes it builds high-order skills and is highly effective, while Robles believes it is ineffective and fails to build soft skills.
questions.expVn: Cả hai tác giả đều đồng ý rằng 'trò chơi hóa' hiện đang được sử dụng trong các tập đoàn (A), liên quan đến các phương pháp phi truyền thống (C), và ban đầu được giới thiệu để tiết kiệm chi phí (D). Tuy nhiên, họ hoàn toàn bất đồng về tính hiệu quả của nó. Morgan tin rằng nó xây dựng các kỹ năng bậc cao và rất hiệu quả, trong khi Robles tin rằng nó không hiệu quả và thất bại trong việc xây dựng kỹ năng mềm.

questions.title: 5. According to Source Material #2, what is one major advantage of game-based learning over traditional classroom training?

options.text: A. It forces employees to compete against each other for the 'employee of the month' perk.
B. It requires no effort or monitoring from the company's managers.
C. It is infinitely repeatable, allowing employees to practice as much as they need.
D. It focuses primarily on developing soft skills and person-to-person communication.

questions.expEn: In the final paragraph of Source Material #2, Morgan states, 'unlike one-time training in a classroom, game-based learning is infinitely repeatable. If employees miss something or need more practice, they can always start the game again...' Option D is incorrect because Robles (not Morgan) discusses soft skills.
questions.expVn: Trong đoạn cuối của Tài liệu #2, Morgan khẳng định, 'khác với việc đào tạo chỉ diễn ra một lần trong lớp học, học tập qua game có thể lặp lại vô hạn. Nếu nhân viên bỏ lỡ điều gì đó hoặc cần thực hành thêm, họ luôn có thể bắt đầu lại trò chơi...' Lựa chọn D sai vì Robles (chứ không phải Morgan) là người thảo luận về kỹ năng mềm.

questions.title: 6. Categorize the following pieces of evidence to support Claim 1 or Claim 2.

bank.text: [A] Video games provide infinitely repeatable practice and feedback to gauge progress.
[B] Gamification fails to develop the "soft skills" necessary for success in any job.
[C] Gamification drives a wedge between younger employees and older workers.
[D] Game-based learning stimulates decision making and problem-solving through challenges.

claims.text: Claim 1: Game-based learning is ineffective and problematic. (Robles)
Claim 2: Game-based learning is a highly beneficial training strategy. (Morgan)

questions.expEn: Evidence [B] and [C] highlight the negative aspects of gamification (lack of soft skills and alienating older workers), which align with Robles' argument in Claim 1. Evidence [A] and [D] highlight the positive benefits (repeatable practice and stimulating higher-order skills), which support Morgan's argument in Claim 2.
questions.expVn: Bằng chứng [B] và [C] nhấn mạnh các khía cạnh tiêu cực của trò chơi hóa (thiếu kỹ năng mềm và làm xa lánh lao động lớn tuổi), phù hợp với lập luận của Robles trong Nhận định 1. Bằng chứng [A] và [D] nhấn mạnh những lợi ích tích cực (thực hành lặp lại và kích thích kỹ năng bậc cao), ủng hộ lập luận của Morgan trong Nhận định 2.

rlaReading.title: Practice 3: Music Review
passage1Paragraphs: Sam "Lightnin'" Hopkins was never what one would call a {perfectionist}. Few of his recordings are free of off-notes or mis-fretted chords, and he had no {compunction} about putting out version after version of the same song.

When attempting to create a "Best of" compilation, one finds that each version has its recommendations, and each its {shortcomings}. There is no objective {yardstick} for selecting what to include. "Katie Mae," for instance, exists in multiple versions. It is yet another instance of having to choose one version that is just as good as (although admittedly no better than) many others.

Consequently, any {purported} "Best of" album is less representative of Sam "Lightnin'" Hopkins than of the person putting it together. All the {intrepid} compiler can do is choose one version of "Mojo Hand" that is just as good as many others.

So it is with this most recent album: the song titles are there, and a fan finds few surprises among them. But do you need this album? Do you need these particular versions, {culled} from various sessions spanning five decades? Do you need yet another "Lightnin'" Hopkins CD titled—you guessed it—MOJO HAND?

perfectionist.def: A person who refuses to accept any standard short of perfection.
perfectionist.vnDef: Người luôn đòi hỏi sự hoàn hảo trong mọi việc.

compunction.def: A feeling of guilt or moral scruple.
compunction.vn: Sự hối hận / Sự e ngại
compunction.vnDef: Cảm giác tội lỗi hoặc ngần ngại khi làm điều gì đó.

shortcomings.def: Faults or failures to meet a certain standard.
shortcomings.vnDef: Những lỗi lầm hoặc sự không đạt tiêu chuẩn.

yardstick.vnDef: Một tiêu chuẩn được sử dụng để so sánh hoặc đánh giá.

purported.def: Appearing or stated to be true, though not necessarily so.
purported.vn: Được cho là / Bị gán cho là
purported.vnDef: Có vẻ như hoặc được cho là đúng, mặc dù không nhất thiết phải như vậy.

intrepid.vnDef: Không sợ hãi; thích phiêu lưu mạo hiểm.

culled.vn: Được chọn lọc / Được nhặt ra
culled.vnDef: Được lựa chọn từ một số lượng lớn.

questions.title: 1. The questions in the final paragraph are intended to make the reader:

options.text: A. wonder if compilation CDs serve any purpose.
B. realize that Hopkins was less talented than many listeners believe.
C. see that this compilation CD may not be a necessary addition to a Hopkins collection.
D. understand that nobody can predict which version of a song will sell the most copies.

questions.expEn: The last paragraph reinforces the reasons given earlier in the passage: With so many versions of a song, all of about equal value, why would someone need these particular, subjectively chosen versions? The author wants readers to think about whether they actually need this album.
questions.expVn: Đoạn cuối củng cố các lý do đã đưa ra trước đó: Với rất nhiều phiên bản của một bài hát có giá trị tương đương nhau, tại sao một người lại cần những phiên bản được chọn lọc theo cảm tính này? Tác giả muốn người đọc tự hỏi liệu họ có thực sự cần album này hay không.

questions.title: 2. The writer describes the compiler (paragraph 3) as being:

options.text: A. misguided.
B. intelligent.
C. artistic.
D. adventurous.

questions.expEn: The author uses the word 'intrepid' to describe the compiler. Because choosing from a long list of roughly equal versions is a subjective gamble, an adventurous or fearless spirit ('intrepid') would be required.
questions.expVn: Tác giả sử dụng từ 'intrepid' (gan dạ/dũng cảm) để mô tả người biên soạn. Vì việc chọn lọc từ một danh sách dài các phiên bản gần như tương đương nhau là một sự đánh cược mang tính chủ quan, nên cần có một tinh thần thích phiêu lưu hoặc không sợ hãi ('intrepid').

questions.title: 3. The author implies that "Katie Mae":

options.text: A. exists in multiple versions.
B. is better than "Lightnin's Boogie."
C. was recorded after "Mojo Hand."
D. has been overshadowed by Hopkins's other songs.

questions.expEn: The passage explicitly states that "Katie Mae" is "yet another instance of having to choose one version that is just as good as... many others." This confirms that it exists in multiple versions.
questions.expVn: Đoạn văn nêu rõ rằng "Katie Mae" là "một ví dụ khác về việc phải chọn một phiên bản tốt tương đương với... nhiều phiên bản khác." Điều này xác nhận rằng bài hát này tồn tại ở nhiều phiên bản.

questions.title: 4. The author infers that Lightnin' Hopkins's body of musical works:

options.text: A. is larger than that of any other blues musician.
B. is filled with too many mistakes for any of the recordings to be considered good.
C. resists easy song selection processes.
D. cannot be represented on a compilation CD.

questions.expEn: The many and roughly equal versions of the same songs make selection difficult, leading to a compilation that is more representative of the person doing the selecting than it is of the singer's work. Therefore, the body of work resists easy selection.
questions.expVn: Nhiều phiên bản của cùng một bài hát gần như tương đương nhau khiến việc chọn lọc trở nên khó khăn, dẫn đến một album tuyển tập phản ánh người chọn bài hơn là phản ánh các tác phẩm của ca sĩ. Do đó, các tác phẩm của ông gây khó khăn cho quá trình chọn lọc bài hát.

rlaReading.title: Practice 4: Historical Science
rlaReading.passageTitle: Killer Clothing Was All the Rage in the 19th Century
passage1Paragraphs: While sitting at home one afternoon in 1861, poet Henry Wadsworth Longfellow's wife, Fanny, caught fire. Her burns were so severe that she died the next day. In the days when candles, oil lamps, and fireplaces lit and heated homes, women's wide hoop skirts and flowing cotton and tulle dresses were a fire hazard.

It wasn't just dresses: Fashion at this time was {riddled} with dangers. Socks made with {aniline} dyes inflamed men's feet. Lead makeup damaged women's wrist nerves. {Celluloid} combs exploded if they got too hot. In fact, some of the most fashionable clothing of the day was made using chemicals that are today considered too {toxic} to use.

Many people think that "mad as a hatter" refers to the mental and physical side effects hat-makers {endured} from using mercury in their craft. In order to make hare and rabbit fur stick together to form felt, hatters brushed it with mercury. One of the first symptoms was {neuromotor} problems, like trembling. Then there were the psychological problems. "You would become very shy, very {paranoid}," Matthews David says. Many hatters also developed {cardiorespiratory} problems, lost their teeth, and died at early ages.

"There was always kind of a bit of a pushback from the hatters themselves," Matthews David says. "But really, honestly, the only thing that made mercury hat-making disappear was the fact that men's hats went out of fashion in the 1960s."

Arsenic was everywhere in Victorian Britain. Because it dyed fabric bright green, arsenic also ended up in dresses, gloves, shoes, and artificial flower wreaths. But like mercury hats, arsenic fashions were most dangerous for the people who manufactured them. For example, in 1861, a 19-year-old artificial flower maker named Matilda Scheurer—whose job involved dusting flowers with green, arsenic-laced powder—died a violent and colorful death. She {convulsed}, vomited, and foamed at the mouth.
Public concern over arsenic helped phase it out of fashion. The move away from arsenic was hastened by the invention of {synthetic} dyes.

This raises interesting questions about fashion today. While arsenic dresses might seem like bizarre {relics} of a more brutal age, killer fashion is still very much in {vogue}. In 2009, Turkey banned sandblasting—the practice of spraying denim with sand to give it a distressed look—because workers were developing {silicosis} from breathing in sand. "If you have sand in your lungs it will kill you."

Yet when a dangerous production method is banned in one country, production typically moves somewhere else. In the 1800s, people might have seen the workers who produced these items on the streets of London. But in a {globalized} economy, many of us don't see the deadly effects that our fashion choices have on others.

riddled.vnDef: Bị lấp đầy bởi một thứ gì đó không mong muốn.

aniline.def: A chemical used to make synthetic dyes.
aniline.vnDef: Một loại hóa chất được sử dụng để làm thuốc nhuộm tổng hợp.

celluloid.vnDef: Một loại nhựa sơ khai rất dễ cháy.

endured.vnDef: Cam chịu một điều gì đó đau đớn hoặc khó khăn.

neuromotor.def: Relating to the nerves and muscles.
neuromotor.vnDef: Liên quan đến các dây thần kinh và cơ bắp.

paranoid.vnDef: Nghi ngờ người khác một cách vô lý.

cardiorespiratory.def: Relating to the heart and lungs.
cardiorespiratory.vnDef: Liên quan đến hệ thống tim mạch và hô hấp.

convulsed.vnDef: Bị những cơn co thắt cơ bắp mạnh mẽ ngoài ý muốn.

synthetic.def: Made by chemical synthesis, especially to imitate a natural product.
synthetic.vnDef: Được tạo ra bằng các phản ứng hóa học để bắt chước một sản phẩm tự nhiên.

relics.def: Objects surviving from an earlier time.
relics.vnDef: Những vật thể còn sót lại từ một thời kỳ trước đó.

vogue.def: The prevailing fashion or style at a particular time.
vogue.vnDef: Phong cách thời trang đang được ưa chuộng.

silicosis.def: Lung disease caused by inhaling silica dust.
silicosis.vnDef: Bệnh phổi do hít phải bụi silica (cát).

globalized.def: Developed to have international influence or operation.
globalized.vnDef: Được phát triển để có sức ảnh hưởng hoặc hoạt động trên quy mô quốc tế.

questions.title: 1. Based on the passage, how would the author likely account for a man who locks his door and checks his windows at night, yet uses the same password for several different online accounts?

options.text: A. He believes there is no security risk involved in online accounts.
B. He feels responsible for safeguarding his tangible assets, but not for intangible cyber assets.
C. A burglar would be a specific person whom the police might catch; an online attacker could be unknown and in any country.
D. He's not aware that reusing the same password makes all of his accounts vulnerable to attack.

questions.expEn: The main idea in the last paragraph emphasizes concern about a local, identifiable danger (like sick workers on the streets) but lack of concern about a danger that is far away and unseen (like a globalized attacker or factory worker). Therefore, the man protects himself from a local burglar he can see, but ignores the distant, unseen cyber threat.
questions.expVn: Ý chính trong đoạn cuối nhấn mạnh sự quan tâm đến một mối nguy hiểm cục bộ, có thể nhận biết được (như những công nhân ốm yếu trên đường phố) nhưng lại thiếu quan tâm đến một mối nguy hiểm ở xa và không nhìn thấy được (như một kẻ tấn công trên mạng toàn cầu). Do đó, người đàn ông bảo vệ mình khỏi một tên trộm địa phương mà anh ta có thể nhìn thấy, nhưng lại bỏ qua mối đe dọa không gian mạng ở xa.

questions.title: 2. One reason the author introduces the present-day fashion industry is to:

options.text: A. show that nothing has changed with respect to unsafe manufacturing techniques.
B. illustrate the effect of globalization.
C. demonstrate how public concern about the welfare of fashion workers has increased.
D. explain how fashion manufacturing has changed since the 1800s.

questions.expEn: The author introduces present-day fashion (sandblasting) to show that dangers still exist, but because of globalization, these dangers simply move to other countries where consumers can't see the deadly effects of their choices.
questions.expVn: Tác giả giới thiệu ngành thời trang hiện đại (phun cát) để chỉ ra rằng những rủi ro vẫn tồn tại, nhưng do toàn cầu hóa, những rủi ro này đơn giản là di chuyển sang các quốc gia khác nơi người tiêu dùng không thể thấy được những hậu quả chết người từ sự lựa chọn của họ.

questions.title: 3. In paragraph 2, the author claims that "fashion at this time was riddled with dangers." What connotations (implications) does the word "riddled" add to her description of fashion?

bank.text: [F] It concerns weakening or damaging something.

questions.expEn: The word 'riddled' reinforces the idea of danger (like being riddled with bullets). It indicates that something is hazardous (but not always deadly like option D suggests). It also portrays fashion as being weakened or damaged by these dangers (like a material weakened by punctures). It does not mean completely 'saturated' or 'hidden'.
questions.expVn: Từ 'riddled' củng cố ý tưởng về sự nguy hiểm (giống như bị thủng lỗ chỗ bởi đạn). Nó chỉ ra rằng có điều gì đó nguy hiểm (nhưng không phải lúc nào cũng chết người như tùy chọn D gợi ý). Nó cũng miêu tả thời trang đang bị suy yếu hoặc bị tổn hại bởi những rủi ro này. Nó không có nghĩa là hoàn toàn 'bão hòa' (saturated) hoặc 'bị giấu kín' (hidden).

questions.title: 4. What primary function do the anecdotes about Fanny Longfellow and Matilda Scheurer serve in the passage?

options.text: A. They introduce humor to the author's tone.
B. They add credibility to the passage.
C. They help the author connect with her readers.
D. They demonstrate how thoroughly the author has researched her topic.

questions.expEn: The vivid focus on a specific person's tragic death gives readers a high-impact, memorable example in a way that a generic description does not. The author connects with her readers through these anecdotes to capture their attention and convince them of the topic's importance.
questions.expVn: Việc tập trung sống động vào cái chết bi thảm của một người cụ thể mang lại cho người đọc một ví dụ đáng nhớ và có tác động mạnh theo cách mà một mô tả chung chung không làm được. Tác giả kết nối với người đọc thông qua những giai thoại này để thu hút sự chú ý của họ và thuyết phục họ về tầm quan trọng của chủ đề.

questions.title: 5. What is the relationship between the author's point of view and her purpose?

options.text: A. The point of view is aligned with the author's purpose.
B. A different point of view would advance her purpose more effectively.
C. Her point of view changes at different points in the passage.
D. The purpose and point of view work against each other.

questions.expEn: The author's purpose is to inform a general audience in an interesting, entertaining way. Her changing point of view (sometimes neutral, authoritative, or mildly critical) is fully consistent and aligned with this purpose of keeping the reader engaged.
questions.expVn: Mục đích của tác giả là thông tin cho độc giả đại chúng một cách thú vị và mang tính giải trí. Góc nhìn thay đổi của bà (đôi khi trung lập, có thẩm quyền, hoặc chỉ trích nhẹ) hoàn toàn nhất quán và phù hợp với mục đích giữ cho người đọc tham gia vào bài viết.

questions.title: 6. As the passage describes the end of dangerous materials and manufacturing techniques, the underlying assumption is that:

options.text: A. consumers preferred fashion over their own safety.
B. the practice was so entrenched that external pressure was required.
C. consumers valued themselves more than they valued the workers who produced fashion garments.
D. fashion workers had no power to bring about change.

questions.expEn: The author notes that mercury hats only ended because hats went out of fashion, and arsenic ended due to public concern and the invention of synthetic dyes. This assumes that without this external pressure or change in fashion, the dangerous practices were so entrenched that they would have continued.
questions.expVn: Tác giả lưu ý rằng mũ thủy ngân chỉ kết thúc vì mũ lỗi mốt, và thạch tín kết thúc do sự lo ngại của công chúng và việc phát minh ra thuốc nhuộm tổng hợp. Điều này giả định rằng nếu không có áp lực từ bên ngoài hoặc sự thay đổi trong thời trang, các hoạt động nguy hiểm này đã ăn sâu đến mức chúng sẽ tiếp tục diễn ra.
